title: Characterization_of_Forced_Flame_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean-Pre

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

**3. Tables:**

*   No tables are present in the provided text.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1 Schematic of a swirl-stabilized, lean-premixed, gas turbine combustor:**

        *   Components:
            *   Siren
            *   Air + fuel
            *   Choked inlet (self-excited)
            *   Swirler
            *   Quartz combustor
            *   Steel combustor
        *   Labels:
            *   PT1
            *   PT2
            *   PT3
            *   PT4
            *   PT5
            *   PTC

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Dimensions (millimeters):
    *   Length: 762-1524
    *   Swirler to combustor plane: 76.2
    *   Between PT3 and PT4: 19.1
    *   Between PT4 and combustor dump plane: 12.7
    *   Between PT3 and combustor dump plane: 50.8
    *   Quartz combustor length: 334.8
    *   From PT5 to end combustor: 109.2
    *   Swirler length: 333.5
    *   Distance between PT4 and PT3: 38.1

*   Mixing Section Dimensions: 0.333 m long, 19.1 mm o.d. centerbody, 38.1 mm i.d. mixing tube.

*   Swirler Angle: 30 deg (flat-vane axial swirler)

*   Sampling Rate: 8192 Hz

*   Data Points per Test: 16,384

*   Pressure: 1 atm
*   Equivalence Ratios: 0.55 to 0.70
*   Nozzle Velocity: 60 to 100 m/s
*   Inlet Temperature: 200 °C
*   Reynolds Number: 33,000-54,000
*   Forcing Frequency: 100-400 Hz

*   PMT Wavelengths:
    *   OH: 307 ± 5 nm
    *   CH: 432 ± 5 nm
    *   CO₂: 365 ± 5 nm

*   CH band pass filter: centered at 430 nm, 10 nm full width at half maximum
* H₂ mole fraction (XH2): =0.00-0.60
* Vinmean: 60 m/s
* Tin: 200°C

**1. Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: XH2 (unitless mole fraction of H2)
        *   y-axis: LCHmax (mm), flame length.
    *   Curves:
        *   Φ = 0.55 (square markers)
        *   Φ = 0.60 (diamond markers)
        *   Φ = 0.65 (triangle markers)
        *   Φ = 0.70 (circle markers)
    *   Key Values:
        *   Shows the dependence of flame length (LCH max )upon H2 mole fraction for Vmean=90 m/s
        *   Data points show the flame length decreases as the H2 mole fraction increases.

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 2:** Deconvoluted stable flame images (CH* chemiluminescence) showing flame shapes for different combinations of Φ and XH2.
    *   Rows (XH2): 0.00, 0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.60
    *   Columns (Φ): 0.55, 0.60, 0.65, 0.70
*   **Figure 3:** Line-of-sight integrated flame images for various XH2 values.
    *   XH2 = 0.00, 0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.60 (top to bottom).

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Operating Conditions (Figures 2 & 3):
    *   Tin = 200 °C
    *   Vmean = 60 m/s (For Figure 3)
    *   Vmean = 90 m/s (For Figure 4)
    *   Φ = 0.55, 0.60, 0.65, 0.70.
    *   XH2 = 0.00, 0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.60.
*   LCH* max: Maximum CH* chemiluminescence intensity point (flame length).
*   α: Flame angle.

title: Characterization_of_Forced_Flame_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean-Pre
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Alpha crit = 36 degrees

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 5(a):** Coordinates of maximum CH* chemiluminescence intensity locations
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: XCH-max (mm)
        *   y-axis: YCH-max (mm)
    *   Curves:
        *   Coordinates of maximum CH* chemiluminescence intensity locations for Flame "A", "B", "C", "D"
    *   Key values:
        *   Temperature (T) = 200 degrees C
        *   Vmean = 60 - 100 m/s
        *   Premixed, XH2 = 0.00-0.60
        *   Equiv. ratio = 0.55-0.70
        *   95 stable flames

*   **Figure 5(b):** Flame angle (alpha) vs the flame length (LCH*max)
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: LCH-max (mm)
        *   y-axis: Flame angle (degree)
    *   Curves:
        *   Flame angle vs. flame length.
    *   Key values:
        *   M-flames location
        *   V-flames location

*   **Figure 6(a):** Comparison of gain of flames A and B: f = 100 Hz
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: V'/Vmean
        *   y-axis: Gain
    *   Curves:
        *   Gain vs. V'/Vmean for Flame "A"
        *   Gain vs. V'/Vmean for Flame "B"
    *   Frequency = 100 Hz

*   **Figure 6(b):** Comparison of gain of flames A and B: f = 250 Hz
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: V'/Vmean
        *   y-axis: Gain
    *   Curves:
        *   Gain vs. V'/Vmean for Flame "A"
        *   Gain vs. V'/Vmean for Flame "B"
    *   Feature: Nonlinear response region marked.
    *   Frequency = 250 Hz

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Input parameters of four different flames**

| Tin (°C) | Vmean (m/s) | Φ     | % H₂ | Tad (K) | Qin (kW) | LCH*max (mm) | α (deg) |

| 200       | 60         | 0.65 | 0    | 1681    | 67.91    | 65.3           | 33      |
| 200       | 70         | 0.70 | 0    | 1767    | 84.90    | 63.5           | 33      |
| 200       | 60         | 0.70 | 15   | 1772    | 72.77    | 55.7           | 35      |
| 200       | 80         | 0.60 | 30   | 1

title: Characterization_of_Forced_Flame_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean-Pre
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
**1. Mathematical equations:**
*  V'/Vmean = 0.13
*  V'/Vmean = 0.40
*  η(ω) = K / | 1 + i2ξ(ω/ωc) – (ω/ωc)² |

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   **Figure 7(a/b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: V'/Vmean (unitless)
        *   y: Gain (unitless)
    *   Curves:
        *   Flame "C" at Frequency = 175 Hz (a) 200 Hz (b)
        *   Flame "D" at Frequency = 175 Hz (a) 200 Hz (b)
    * Key values:
        *V'/Vmean ranges from 0 to 0.8
        *Gain ranges from 0 to approximately 2.8

*   **Figure 8(a):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Frequency (Hz)
        *   y: Gain (unitless)
    *   Curves:
        *   0% H2 (measurement): increase -> peak -> decrease, maximum Gain around 2.5 at some 250 Hz,
        *   0% H2 (empirical fit): increase -> peak -> decrease
        *   15% H2 (measurement): increase-> peak -> decrease, less than 1.3.
        *   15% H2 (empirical fit): increase -> peak -> decrease

*   **Figure 8(b):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Frequency (Hz)
        *   y: Phase (rad)
    *   Curves:
        *   0% H2: increase steadily to about 4 rad
        *   15% H2: increase steadily to less than 2 rad

**3. Tables:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   f = 250 Hz
*   f = 200 Hz, 175 Hz
*   V'/Vmean = 0.100
*   Tin = 200 °C
*   Vmean = 60 m/s
*   phi = 0.60
*   XH2 = 0.00, 0.15, 0% и 15%
*   Tad = 1591K, 1596K
*  Qin =62.99kW 和 63.06kW
*   Frequency ranges from 0 to 500 Hz

*   **Figure 11(b):**
